title: Schradera scandens
taxonomy: Schradera scandens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is native to tropical Asia, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other Rubiaceae species. The Rubiaceae family, commonly known as the madder or coffee family, is a large and diverse group of plants. Schradera scandens is one of the many species within this family, characterized by its unique characteristics and adaptations. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Schradera scandens shares many similarities with other plants in this group.

intro: Schradera scandens, also known as climbing Schradera, is a climbing shrub native to tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Schradera scandens contains bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, including infusions and decoctions, as well as being used in the formulation of topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
